# Deploy OpenClaw on AWS EC2

Deploy [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw) — an open-source AI agent that runs 24/7 — on a standalone AWS EC2 instance. Uses Terragrunt and OpenTofu following the [Gruntwork infrastructure-live pattern](https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt-infrastructure-live-stacks-example).

## What you'll deploy

You'll deploy a self-contained Terragrunt configuration that creates:

1. A dedicated **VPC** with a public subnet and internet gateway
2. An **EC2 instance** (Graviton ARM by default) running OpenClaw in Docker
3. An **IAM role** for **SSM Session Manager** — shell access and port forwarding with no open ports
4. A **security group** with no inbound ports (all access is via SSM)

Access to OpenClaw is via **SSM port forwarding**, which tunnels the OpenClaw UI to `localhost` on your machine. Authentication is handled by your AWS credentials — no VPN, SSH keys, or open ports needed.

Estimated monthly cost: **~$27/month** (t4g.medium + 30GB EBS).

## Pre-flight checks

Before you begin, ensure you have the prerequisites below, the required tools installed, and are properly authenticated.

### Prerequisites

- **Dedicated AWS account:** We recommend deploying OpenClaw in a dedicated AWS account to keep it isolated from other workloads. If you don't have one yet, create one in your AWS Organization before proceeding.
- **infra-live repo:** You need an **infra-live** repository with a `root.hcl` and the standard Terragrunt folder hierarchy. If you don't have one yet, create one first by running:
  ```
  runbooks open https://github.com/gruntwork-io/runbooks/tree/main/testdata/sample-runbooks/new-infra-live-repo
  ```
  That runbook also includes an optional step to create an infra-catalog repo.
- **infra-catalog repo:** You need an **infra-catalog** repository where reusable OpenTofu/Terraform modules are stored. If you don't have one, create an empty repo on GitHub before proceeding.

### Authenticate to GitHub

Authenticate to GitHub so the runbook can clone repos, create pull requests, and create releases.

<GitHubAuth
  id="gh-auth"
  title="Authenticate to GitHub"
  description="Authenticate to GitHub to access your infra-catalog and infra-live repositories."
/>

### Authenticate to AWS

**Authenticate to the AWS account where OpenClaw will run.** We recommend using a dedicated AWS account for OpenClaw to keep it isolated from other workloads.

<AwsAuth
  id="aws-auth"
  title="Authenticate to AWS"
  description="Authenticate to the AWS account where you want to deploy OpenClaw."
  defaultRegion="us-west-2"
/>

### Detect AWS Account ID

<Command
  id="detect-account"
  path="scripts/detect-account.sh"
  title="Detect AWS Account ID"
  description="Fetch the AWS account ID from your authenticated session."
  successMessage="Account ID detected! It will be prefilled in the configuration form below."
  failMessage="Failed to detect account ID. You can enter it manually."
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

### Install mise

Verify that mise is installed. If not, install it from [mise.jdx.dev](https://mise.jdx.dev/getting-started.html).

<Check
  id="check-mise"
  command="mise --version && mise self-update --yes;"
  title="Check mise Installation"
  description="We recommend `mise` as a tool version manager that can install and manage Terragrunt, OpenTofu, and other tools. This checks that mise is installed and up to date."
  successMessage="mise is installed and up to date!"
  failMessage="mise is not installed. Install it from https://mise.jdx.dev/getting-started.html"
/>

### Install Required Tools

Once mise is installed, run the following command to install all required tools:

<Command
  id="install-tools"
  command="mise use terragrunt@latest opentofu@latest awscli@latest granted@latest"
  title="Install Required Tools via mise"
  description="Installs Terragrunt, OpenTofu, AWS CLI, and Granted using mise"
  successMessage="All tools installed successfully!"
  failMessage="Failed to install tools. Check the logs for details."
/>

### Install AWS Session Manager Plugin

The Session Manager plugin for the AWS CLI is required to connect to the EC2 instance. Install it from [the AWS docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/session-manager-working-with-install-plugin.html), or on macOS:

```bash
brew install --cask session-manager-plugin
```

<Check
  id="check-ssm-plugin"
  command="session-manager-plugin --version"
  title="Check Session Manager Plugin"
  description="Verify that the AWS Session Manager plugin is installed."
  successMessage="Session Manager plugin is installed!"
  failMessage="Session Manager plugin is not installed. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/session-manager-working-with-install-plugin.html"
/>

## infra-catalog: Generate the OpenTofu Module

### Clone infra-catalog

Clone the **infra-catalog** repo where the OpenTofu module will be written.

<GitClone
  id="clone-catalog"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone infra-catalog"
  description="Clone the repository where the OpenTofu module will be written."

/>

### Configure and generate the OpenTofu module

<Template
  id="gen-module"
  path="templates/infra-catalog-module"
  target="worktree"
/>

### Create a pull request on infra-catalog

Push the generated module and open a pull request.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-catalog"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  inputsId="gen-module"
  title="Create Pull Request on infra-catalog"
  description="Push the generated OpenTofu module and open a PR against infra-catalog."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Add {{ .inputs.ModuleName }} module"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="Add the {{ .inputs.ModuleName }} OpenTofu module.\n\nGenerated by the OpenClaw runbook."
  prefilledBranchName="add-{{ .inputs.ModuleName }}-module"
/>

### Merge the pull request

Review and merge the pull request on GitHub, then verify it's been merged.

<Check
  id="check-pr-merged"
  path="checks/check-pr-merged.sh"
  title="Verify PR is Merged"
  description="Check that the infra-catalog pull request has been merged to main."
  successMessage="PR is merged!"
  failMessage="PR is not yet merged. Merge it on GitHub and try again."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

### Create a release on infra-catalog

Tag the infra-catalog repo so Terragrunt can pull a pinned version of the module.

<Command
  id="release-catalog"
  path="scripts/release-catalog.sh"
  title="Create Release"
  description="Create a release on the infra-catalog repo."
  successMessage="Release created!"
  failMessage="Failed to create release. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId="gen-module"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

## infra-live: Generate the Terragrunt Unit

### Clone infra-live

Clone the **infra-live** repo where the Terragrunt unit (terragrunt.hcl) will be written.

<GitClone
  id="clone-live"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone infra-live"
  description="Clone the repository where the Terragrunt unit (terragrunt.hcl) will be written."

/>

### Detect root.hcl

Check if the infra-live repo contains a `root.hcl`. If found, the generated `terragrunt.hcl` will automatically include it.

<Check
  id="check-root-hcl"
  path="checks/check-root-hcl.sh"
  title="Detect root.hcl"
  description="Check if root.hcl exists in the infra-live repo."
  successMessage="root.hcl found — it will be included in the generated terragrunt.hcl."
  warnMessage="No root.hcl found — the generated terragrunt.hcl will be self-contained."
/>

### Generate the Terragrunt unit into infra-live

<Template
  id="gen-unit"
  path="templates/infra-live-unit"
  target="worktree"
  inputsId="gen-module"
/>

### Create a pull request on infra-live

Push the generated Terragrunt unit and open a pull request.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-live"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  inputsId="gen-unit"
  title="Create Pull Request on infra-live"
  description="Push the generated Terragrunt unit and open a PR against infra-live."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Add {{ .inputs.InstanceName }} Terragrunt unit"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="Add Terragrunt configuration for {{ .inputs.InstanceName }}.\n\nGenerated by the OpenClaw runbook."
  prefilledBranchName="add-{{ .inputs.InstanceName }}-unit"
/>

### Merge the infra-live pull request

Review and merge the pull request on GitHub, then verify it's been merged.

<Check
  id="check-live-pr-merged"
  path="checks/check-live-pr-merged.sh"
  title="Verify infra-live PR is Merged"
  description="Check that the infra-live pull request has been merged to main."
  successMessage="PR is merged!"
  failMessage="PR is not yet merged. Merge it on GitHub and try again."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

## Deploy OpenClaw

Preview the changes with a plan, then apply.

<Command
  id="terragrunt-plan"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-plan.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Plan"
  description="Preview the infrastructure changes that will be made to deploy OpenClaw."
  successMessage="Plan completed successfully! Review the changes above."
  failMessage="Terragrunt plan failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

<Command
  id="terragrunt-apply"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-apply.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Apply"
  description="Apply the infrastructure changes to deploy OpenClaw on EC2."
  successMessage="OpenClaw deployed successfully!"
  failMessage="Terragrunt apply failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

## Access OpenClaw

OpenClaw is accessed via **SSM port forwarding**, which securely tunnels the OpenClaw UI to `localhost` on your machine. No VPN or open ports needed — your AWS credentials handle authentication.

<Command
  id="instance-info"
  path="scripts/save-ssh-key.sh"
  title="Get Connection Info"
  description="Retrieve the instance ID and connection commands."
  successMessage="Connection info retrieved!"
  failMessage="Failed to retrieve connection info. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

### 1. Start the port forward

Run this command in a **dedicated terminal window** — it needs to stay running while you use OpenClaw. Save it somewhere handy so you can restart it if your machine reboots.

<TemplateInline id="port-forward-snippet">
```bash
{{ .outputs.instance_info.ssm_port_forward_command }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### 2. Open OpenClaw

Once the port forward is running, open your browser to:

```
http://localhost:18789
```

### 3. Retrieve the gateway password

When prompted for a password, retrieve it via SSM:

<TemplateInline id="password-snippet">
```bash
{{ .outputs.instance_info.ssm_password_command }}
```
</TemplateInline>

Enter the password in the OpenClaw UI and click **Connect**.

### 4. Configure your Anthropic API key

OpenClaw needs an Anthropic API key to power its AI agent. Get one from [console.anthropic.com/settings/keys](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys) if you don't have one already.

Open a shell on the instance and configure the key:

<TemplateInline id="apikey-snippet">
```bash
{{ .outputs.instance_info.ssm_command }}
```
</TemplateInline>

Then on the instance, run:

```bash
sudo docker exec openclaw openclaw config set agents.main.provider.apiKey "sk-ant-your-key-here"
sudo docker restart openclaw
```

Replace `sk-ant-your-key-here` with your actual API key. The key is stored in the container's config file and persists across restarts.

### 5. Monitor cloud-init progress (optional)

If you just deployed and OpenClaw isn't responding yet, cloud-init may still be installing packages. You can monitor progress:

<TemplateInline id="monitor-snippet">
```bash
{{ .outputs.instance_info.ssm_command }}
# Then on the instance:
tail -f /var/log/cloud-init-output.log
```
</TemplateInline>

## Verify the Deployment

<Check
  id="test-openclaw"
  path="checks/test-openclaw.sh"
  title="Verify OpenClaw Deployment"
  description="Check that the EC2 instance is running and OpenClaw is operational."
  successMessage="OpenClaw is deployed and running!"
  failMessage="Verification failed. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId="gen-unit"
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

## Cleanup (Optional)

When you're done with OpenClaw, you can tear down all the infrastructure created by this runbook. This will delete the EC2 instance, VPC, security group, and IAM role.

<Command
  id="terragrunt-destroy"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-destroy.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Destroy"
  description="Destroy all OpenClaw infrastructure. This action is irreversible."
  successMessage="All OpenClaw resources have been destroyed."
  failMessage="Terragrunt destroy failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>
